combined_devices.limitations: Measures only grip (single sub-construct); less comprehensive than full HHD megascore for overall muscle function assessment; longitudinal sensitivity highly variable between participants (PMID:37219865); does not capture lower extremity or proximal weakness; frequently used as secondary/exploratory rather than anchoring the trial design; no standalone MID established

combined_devices.manufacturer: unknown (not specified in corpus; described as fitness-device form factor for home use)
combined_devices.wear_location: Surface electrodes placed on target muscle
measures_supported: Muscle impedance parameters (reactance, resistance, phase angle)
combined_devices.limitations: Not a direct measure of muscle strength (measures tissue impedance properties as proxy for muscle health); longitudinal sensitivity results from methodology study (NCT02611674, N=138) not available in corpus; used only as exploratory/methodology outcome; no Phase 2/3 trial has used EIM as secondary or primary endpoint for muscle function; relationship between impedance change and clinically meaningful strength change not established in corpus

Single cross-sectional study (N=54) providing clinical validation; no longitudinal change-score data; no trial endpoint use; single-center specialist clinic setting; correlates with split-hand index supporting biological plausibility but not endpoint readiness; measures only distal hand sub-construct

combined_devices.manufacturer: Various (specific devices not named in corpus)
combined_devices.wear_location: Wrist or body (not specified in corpus)
measures_supported: Activity as indirect motor function proxy
combined_devices.limitations: Indirect/proxy measure of muscle function (physical activity is downstream consequence of strength, not direct measurement); systematic review noted high risk of bias across accelerometry studies in MND; no specific accelerometer device validated as muscle function endpoint in ALS; construct validity for muscle_function COI specifically is undemonstrated; all studies showed only initial feasibility

combined_gaps.description: The most validated ROM measurement platform (Microsoft Kinect for RWS/RSA) is commercially discontinued. No successor 3D depth sensor has been validated for RWS in ALS.
combined_gaps.action: Identify currently-available 3D depth sensors (Intel RealSense, Apple LiDAR, Azure successor) and assess whether cross-validation studies with the original Kinect-based RSA exist or are planned. Contact RWS research groups (authors of PMID:27813421, PMID:25965847) to determine which hardware they have migrated to.

combined_gaps.description: No registered RCT in ALS uses sensor-based ROM (RWS/RSA or IMU-derived) as a primary or secondary endpoint. All use is exploratory or observational.
combined_gaps.action: Search ClinicalTrials.gov for any Phase 2/3 ALS trial with 'reachable workspace' or 'sensor-based ROM' in primary/secondary outcomes. If none found, this remains a qualification gap requiring prospective endpoint qualification study design.

combined_gaps.description: No MID (Minimal Important Difference) or MICD established for RWS/RSA or any sensor-based ROM measure in ALS. The 19% annual decline in upper lateral quadrant RSA (PMID:27813421) provides a rate estimate but no anchor-based MID.
combined_gaps.action: Determine whether any ongoing study is deriving anchor-based MID for RSA using ALSFRS-R meaningful change or patient global impression. Required before any primary/secondary endpoint use.

combined_gaps.description: All ALS ROM validation studies have small samples (N=10-13 patients). No study exceeds N=17 total (including controls). Power calculations for detecting treatment effects are absent.
combined_gaps.action: Identify any larger cohort studies using sensor-based ROM in ALS or adjacent NMDs (DMD, FSHD where RWS is also validated per PMID:41960733). Determine sample size requirements for a secondary endpoint based on observed effect sizes and variability.

combined_gaps.description: No FDA or EMA qualification opinion, Biomarker Development Data (BDD) submission, or endpoint guidance document specifically endorses sensor-based ROM measurement in ALS or NMD.
combined_gaps.action: Check FDA DDT qualification submissions database and EMA qualification opinions for any ROM/RWS-related submissions. Review FSHD and DMD endpoint guidance for cross-indication precedent given RWS validation across these diseases (referenced in PMID:41960733).

combined_gaps.description: No specific commercial IMU product is named or validated for ALS head-neck ROM measurement. Studies use generic 'inertial sensors' without product identification, preventing reproducibility and regulatory traceability.
combined_gaps.action: Contact authors of PMID:28068376 to identify specific IMU hardware used. Search for commercial IMU-based head motion systems (e.g., Xsens, APDM/Clario) and determine if any have ALS-specific validation data not captured in this corpus.

combined_gaps.description: No remote or home-based validation exists for any ROM measurement DHT in ALS. All studies are clinic-based, supervised, single-site.
combined_gaps.action: Determine whether the 'remote monitoring' applications mentioned in PMID:41960733 have any published pilot data. Assess feasibility of home-based RWS measurement using consumer depth sensors or IMU systems.

combined_gaps.description: The narrative review (PMID:41960733) references 'more than a decade of experience in clinical studies' and validation in multiple NMDs. The full body of RWS evidence (particularly in DMD and FSHD) is not captured in this ALS-focused corpus and may contain cross-indication validation data relevant to regulatory qualification.
combined_gaps.action: Retrieve the full citation list from PMID:41960733 and identify cross-NMD longitudinal studies, especially any with sample sizes >50 or RCT use, to strengthen the overall RWS evidence package.

combined_gaps.description: No MID/MICD (minimal important difference / minimal important clinical difference) established for any oculometric measure in ALS. Without MID, sample size calculations for primary/secondary endpoint use lack an anchor, and individual patient meaningful change cannot be defined.
combined_gaps.action: Anchor-based MID study needed linking oculometric change scores to patient-meaningful ALSFRS-R decline thresholds or clinician global impression of change. Distribution-based estimates (SEM, 0.5 SD) could provide interim values.

combined_gaps.description: No FDA or EMA guidance identifies oculomotor function as a recognized or encouraged endpoint in ALS. No Drug Development Tool (DDT) qualification, no Biomarker Qualification submission, no Clinical Outcome Assessment qualification found for oculometric measures in ALS.
combined_gaps.action: Search FDA DDT database, EMA qualification opinions, and agency meeting minutes for any oculomotor/eye-tracking biomarker discussions in neurodegenerative disease. If absent, a Letter of Intent for biomarker qualification or pre-submission meeting may be needed before non-exploratory endpoint use.

combined_gaps.description: Full results from NCT05407428 (N=45, completed) not yet published as peer-reviewed paper in this corpus. This is the largest NeuraLight ALS study and includes comparison with a validated eye-tracking system—results would substantially strengthen or weaken the evidence base.
combined_gaps.action: Check ClinicalTrials.gov results posting; contact NeuraLight for publication timeline or data availability; search preprint servers for interim results.

combined_gaps.description: No remote/home-based validation exists for NeuraLight platform. All clinical data collected in clinic settings during supervised trial visits. The potential for reduced clinic visits (referenced in PMID:39015513 for EyePhone) is undemonstrated for the platform with actual clinical validation.
combined_gaps.action: Determine whether NeuraLight platform supports unsupervised home use; if so, request home-vs-clinic agreement data or propose a substudy with paired assessments.

combined_gaps.description: No independent replication of NeuraLight oculometric findings outside of NeuraLight-involved or NeuraLight-sponsored studies. All clinical validation data in this corpus involves the NeuraLight platform with apparent company involvement.
combined_gaps.action: Search for independent academic publications using NeuraLight or replicating oculometric-ALSFRS-R correlations with other platforms. If none exist, weight this as a replication gap in evidence assessment.

combined_gaps.description: The 'validated eye-tracking system' used as comparator in NCT05407428 is not named in the corpus. Identity unknown—could be a clinical-grade VOG system, research-grade eye tracker (e.g., Tobii, EyeLink), or another platform. This comparator's identity and agreement results are needed to interpret NeuraLight's concurrent validity.
combined_gaps.action: Review NCT05407428 protocol or publications for named comparator device; contact NeuraLight or PI for device identity and agreement metrics.

combined_gaps.description: Test-retest reliability (ICC) not reported for any oculometric measure in ALS patients in this corpus. Without known measurement error, SEM cannot be calculated and change scores cannot be distinguished from noise.
combined_gaps.action: Request test-retest data from NeuraLight (may exist from NCT05407428 repeated assessments); alternatively, derive from within-patient variance in longitudinal dataset (PMID:38975625).

combined_gaps.description: NeuraLight algorithm versioning and lock status unknown. Whether the oculometric extraction algorithms used in PMID:37026395 (2023) and PMID:38975625 (2024) are identical, and whether a locked version exists for regulatory submission, is not stated.
combined_gaps.action: Request algorithm version history and lock status from NeuraLight; confirm whether trial data were analyzed with a prospectively locked algorithm or retrospectively reprocessed.

combined_gaps.description: Corpus limited to 4 records. Recall-recovery passes (PI-branded searches, methods-section naming, adjacent-indication oculomotor studies) were not performed. Additional devices (e.g., Tobii, SR Research EyeLink, Natus/Micromedical VOG systems, other digital platforms) may have ALS-relevant evidence not captured here.
combined_gaps.action: Expand search to include: (1) research-grade eye trackers used in ALS studies named only in methods sections; (2) other commercial oculomotor platforms (e.g., C2N, Neurotrack for related constructs); (3) adjacent indications (FTD, PD, MS) with oculomotor validation that may transfer.

combined_gaps.description: No established Minimal Important Difference (MID) or Minimal Clinically Important Difference (MICD) for HHD megascore or any dynamometry-based muscle strength measure in ALS
combined_gaps.action: Conduct anchor-based MID estimation using existing Phase 3 trial datasets (tofersen VALOR, FORTITUDE-ALS, ceftriaxone) with ALSFRS-R domain change or patient global impression as anchor

combined_gaps.description: ATLIS regulatory clearance status and commercial availability unclear; cannot confirm whether trial sites can procure it as a standard device
combined_gaps.action: Verify FDA/CE regulatory status of ATLIS system; determine manufacturer, pricing, and commercial availability for multi-site trial deployment

combined_gaps.description: Portable Fixed Dynamometer (PFD) not yet tested as endpoint in Phase 2/3 clinical trial; pilot data only (N=18 longitudinal)
combined_gaps.action: Design multi-site validation study comparing PFD longitudinal sensitivity head-to-head with clinic-based HHD and ATLIS; establish manufacturer pathway for commercial availability

combined_gaps.description: No home-based or remote muscle strength measurement validated as secondary/primary endpoint in a completed Phase 2/3 ALS trial; clinic-based assessment dominates
combined_gaps.action: Evaluate PFD or equivalent unsupervised fixed dynamometry as remote endpoint; leverage daily/biweekly measurement frequency advantage for sample size reduction (per PMID:32515889 proof-of-concept)

combined_gaps.description: Published comparative longitudinal sensitivity results from Biogen methodology study (NCT02611674) comparing standardized response means of EIM, HHD, MUNE, MUNIX not available in corpus
combined_gaps.action: Retrieve published results from NCT02611674 to determine which electrophysiological and strength measures show largest standardized mean change over 6-12 months

combined_gaps.description: No FDA or EMA formal qualification of HHD megascore or any dynamometry composite as a drug development tool (DDT) biomarker endpoint for ALS
combined_gaps.action: Review FDA Biomarker Qualification Program and EMA qualification opinion archives for ALS muscle strength submissions; identify whether Letter of Support exists for dynamometry

combined_gaps.description: Specific HHD device models and manufacturers used across major Phase 3 trials not consistently reported; cross-brand measurement equivalence not verified in corpus
combined_gaps.action: Audit methods sections of key trials (tofersen, ceftriaxone, FORTITUDE) for exact HHD model; search for cross-device agreement studies

combined_gaps.description: Standard Error of Measurement (SEM) for HHD megascore in multi-site ALS trial settings not reported in this corpus; only PFD provides SEM (5.8%)
combined_gaps.action: Extract test-retest reliability and SEM estimates from completed multi-site Phase 3 trials or NCT02611674 methodology study for HHD

combined_gaps.description: ATLIS demonstrated sample size reduction of approximately one-third vs. ALSFRS-R but only in single 5-site study (N=66 complete data); replication needed
combined_gaps.action: Confirm ATLIS sample size advantage in independent dataset or AMX0035 trial (NCT03488524) published results

combined_evidence.clinical_definition: Range of motion (ROM) in ALS encompasses the progressive loss of joint excursion due to motor neuron degeneration and resulting muscle weakness. Key sub-constructs include upper extremity reachable workspace (quantified as relative surface area, RSA), head-neck movement impairment (including head drop from cervical muscle weakness), and lower-limb joint kinematics (notably ankle dorsiflexion loss). The most operationalized DHT-based measure is 3D sensor-derived reachable workspace RSA, which integrates multi-joint ROM into a single functional metric.

gate_rationale.disease_relevance: ROM decline is directly demonstrated in ALS with significant differences vs. controls (p<0.001 for RSA, PMID:25965847) and longitudinal decline of ~19%/year (PMID:27813421); head-neck ROM impairment objectively characterized (PMID:28068376); ankle ROM reduced 10° in MND (DOI:10.1080/21678421.2025.2523945). RWS explicitly designated a COA tool for ALS (PMID:41960733).
gate_rationale.regulatory_precedent: No FDA qualification, EMA qualification opinion, or endpoint guidance specifically endorsing sensor-based ROM in ALS was identified in this corpus. RWS is characterized as a COA tool in a 2026 narrative review (PMID:41960733) but without cited regulatory acceptance. NCT06363357 uses 'Active Range of motion' and 'Functional workspace' as registered outcome measures in neuromuscular disease, establishing trial-use precedent.
gate_rationale.sensor_maturity: The most validated sensor platform (Microsoft Kinect) is commercially discontinued. The measurement concept (RWS/RSA) is mature and validated, but no currently-commercial device is specifically validated for this endpoint in ALS within this corpus. IMUs are generic mature technology but no ALS-specific validated commercial product identified. Field is in acknowledged transition to mobile sensing (PMID:41960733).

measurement_spec.primary_metric: Relative Surface Area (RSA) of upper extremity reachable workspace, normalized by arm length; secondary metrics include joint-specific angular ROM (degrees), angular velocity, movement smoothness (spectral arc length), and movement coupling indices
measurement_spec.epoch: Single standardized assessment session; RWS requires bilateral arm sweep protocol; head-neck requires 5 repetitions of single-plane cycles at self-selected speed
measurement_spec.algorithm: RWS: 3D depth sensor tracks arm endpoints during maximal reach, reconstructs surface envelope, normalizes area by arm-length-squared sphere. Head-neck: IMU-derived Euler angles for flexion-extension, lateral bending, axial rotation. No locked validated algorithm version identified for any platform in corpus.
measurement_spec.valid_day_rule: Not established in this corpus; PMID:27813421 used single-session visits at intervals; no minimum wear-time or multi-day aggregation rule defined

combined_evidence.clinical_definition: Oculomotor function in ALS encompasses measurable eye movement parameters—saccades (pro-saccade gain, peak velocity, latency), smooth pursuit (fixation time), anti-saccade error rate, and saccadic intrusions (square wave jerks: frequency, amplitude, duration)—that serve as potential objective biomarkers of disease progression, particularly correlating with bulbar functional decline as measured by the ALSFRS-R.

gate_rationale.disease_relevance: Significant correlations between oculometric measures and ALSFRS-R/bulbar subscale demonstrated in ALS patients (R=0.52 corrective saccadic latency, R=-0.43 to -0.47 SWJ parameters vs bulbar subscale), with longitudinal change detection over trial duration (PMID:37026395, PMID:38975625)
gate_rationale.regulatory_precedent: Oculometric measures used as exploratory endpoints within Phase IIb drug trials but no primary/secondary endpoint acceptance, no FDA/EMA guidance naming oculomotor as a recognized ALS endpoint identified in this corpus (PMID:37026395, PMID:38975625)
gate_rationale.sensor_maturity: Software-based oculometric platforms can extract validated measures from standard eye-tracking hardware; smartphone-based acquisition demonstrated feasible for self-administration (NCT05407428, PMID:39015513)

measurement_spec.primary_metric: Square wave jerk frequency and amplitude (longitudinal sensitivity demonstrated); corrective saccadic latency (strongest single cross-sectional correlation R=0.52); pro-saccade gain and peak velocity; anti-saccade error rate; smooth pursuit fixation time
measurement_spec.epoch: Not standardized in available evidence; assessments conducted at trial visit intervals (specific inter-assessment interval not reported)
measurement_spec.algorithm: Software-based extraction of oculometric parameters during presentation of visual stimuli (fixation, pro-saccade, anti-saccade, smooth pursuit tasks); proprietary NeuraLight algorithms for saccadic intrusion detection and classification
measurement_spec.valid_day_rule: Not established; no minimum recording or valid-assessment criteria reported in corpus

combined_evidence.clinical_definition: Muscle function in ALS encompasses progressive loss of voluntary isometric muscle strength due to upper and lower motor neuron degeneration. Quantitative assessment via dynamometry (hand-held, fixed, grip-specific) provides objective, continuous measures of disease progression across upper and lower extremities, correlating strongly with functional capacity (ALSFRS-R fine motor rs=0.740, gross motor similarly) and quality of life. Sub-constructs include individual muscle group strength, composite megascores, grip strength, pinch strength, and neck flexion strength as a respiratory predictor.

gate_rationale.disease_relevance: Progressive muscle weakness is the cardinal clinical feature of ALS; grip strength strongly correlates with ALSFRS-R fine motor (rs=0.740) and ADL (rs=-0.637), supporting it as a meaningful outcome (PMID:42084479, PMID:37641579)
gate_rationale.regulatory_precedent: HHD megascore used as secondary endpoint in multiple Phase 3 RCTs including tofersen (PMID:36129998), reldesemtiv (NCT03160898, NCT04944784), masitinib (NCT03127267), and dazucorilant (NCT05407324)
gate_rationale.sensor_maturity: Dynamometry technology is commercially mature with established test-retest reliability (ICC >0.95), standardized multi-site protocols validated across large ALS trial networks (PMID:27385750, PMID:37427714, PMID:33355879)

measurement_spec.primary_metric: Isometric muscle strength megascore (mean of percent-predicted values across bilateral testing of 9+ muscle groups) or individual muscle MVIC in Newtons
measurement_spec.epoch: Per-visit assessment every 4-12 weeks in clinic trials; biweekly for home-based fixed dynamometry
measurement_spec.algorithm: Maximum of 2-3 MVIC attempts per muscle group; megascore = average of scaled strength measures producing overall composite; decline expressed as slope (linear mixed-effects model) over time
measurement_spec.valid_day_rule: Single-session assessment with trained evaluator for HHD; consecutive-day test-retest for reliability estimation; unsupervised acceptable for fixed dynamometry where equivalence demonstrated (ICC ≥0.97)
